content:
Member-only story

6 min read
1 hour ago

--

Stop wiring streams to more streams. Signals make most RxJS patterns unnecessary. Read this if you ever felt RxJS was clever but heavy.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Why this matters right now
RxJS feels powerful until the app grows. Observables scatter across services and components. Subscriptions leak. Debugging becomes a scavenger hunt. Signals solve the same problems with simpler primitives and fewer moving parts. This article will show seven precise, battle-tested tricks that will replace common RxJS use cases. Each trick contains a short problem statement, a clean code change, and a micro-benchmark or measurable outcome.
TL;DR the seven tricks

• Local derived state instead of map/switchMap chains

• Lightweight sharing with computed vs shareReplay

• Replace Subjects with writable signals

• Debounce at render with memo + timer, not with complex operators

• Error boundaries using try/catch + derived flags, not error channels

• Simple retry logic in an async service wrapper

• Global store with signals instead of BehaviorSubject towers
